model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed step budget, doubling batch size lets the model see substantially more training examples within the same 256 updates; a small learning-rate increase keeps the larger-batch optimization from becoming too conservative.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last improvement came from exposing the model to more examples per fixed update budget; pushing batch size to 256 should cover more than a full epoch within 256 steps, and scaling the learning rate keeps progress per update from flattening out.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"At the current larger-batch, multi-epoch-equivalent regime, the model is no longer purely optimization-limited; a small amount of label smoothing often lowers validation cross-entropy on CIFAR-10 with essentially no runtime cost.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last regularization change clearly hurt validation loss, so the best next move is to remove it and recover the stronger optimization-focused baseline before trying a different direction.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization setup is now much stronger than the original baseline, so a modest width increase is a plausible next gain source; raising the base width from 192/384-equivalent hidden sizes to 256/512 adds capacity without changing the overall training recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After a modest gain from widening, the next clean capacity increase is one additional hidden block; with the current width settings this yields a 256-512-1024 MLP, which is a plausible improvement under the now much stronger optimization regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After several capacity increases, validation improvements are getting small, which often means the current step size is a bit aggressive for the deeper, wider network; a modest LR reduction is a clean next calibration.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"            return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current setup is already training effectively, so a cleaner cosine decay to zero is a good next refinement; removing the 10% learning-rate floor should let the larger model settle more in the final part of the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        warmup_steps = max(1, TRAIN_MAX_STEPS // 20)","old":"        warmup_steps = max(1, TRAIN_MAX_STEPS // 10)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Now that the schedule shape is better, the next likely inefficiency is spending too much of a short 256-step budget in warmup; cutting warmup from 10% to 5% should let the model exploit the stronger LR earlier while keeping the decay behavior intact.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        warmup_steps = max(1, TRAIN_MAX_STEPS // 10)","old":"        warmup_steps = max(1, TRAIN_MAX_STEPS // 20)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The shorter warmup clearly hurt validation loss, so the next step is to restore the stronger 10% warmup and keep the better zero-floor cosine schedule while exploring a different axis afterward.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"This workload is heavily budget-limited, and AdamW with a very high second-moment beta can adapt too slowly over only 256 steps; lowering beta2 is a cheap way to make the optimizer more responsive without changing the broader recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering beta2 from 0.999 to 0.98 helped, which suggests this short-budget run benefits from a faster-moving second-moment estimate; pushing it slightly further to 0.95 is a focused follow-up on the same successful direction.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only about 1.3 effective epochs and a steadily improved optimizer, the current model may now be slightly over-regularized; trimming weight decay is a cheap way to test whether validation loss can drop further without changing runtime.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current recipe is close, and with the more responsive AdamW betas a slightly smaller peak step size may reduce late-training overshoot and improve validation loss without affecting runtime.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The slight LR reduction moved the result in the wrong direction, so the best next step is to restore the stronger 2.5e-3 setting and preserve the better optimizer and schedule changes.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"delete_exact","source":"","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 5.0)"}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run is now stable and well-tuned, so the remaining global gradient clipping may be unnecessarily constraining updates; removing it is a simple way to test whether optimization can sharpen further without adding runtime or complexity.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The biggest gains came from letting the fixed 256-step budget cover more training examples, and the current run still sees only about 1.3 effective epochs; increasing batch size to 512 is the cleanest remaining way to push that further without changing the tuned optimizer and schedule.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The jump to batch size 512 was by far the strongest improvement, and larger batches often prefer a somewhat larger step size; increasing the base LR is the highest-leverage follow-up that preserves the now clearly successful recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate degraded the strong batch-512 configuration, so the best final step is to restore the better 2.5e-3 setting while keeping the major gain from the larger batch size and improved AdamW betas.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}
